title: Fix aria2 large-file download tail collapse (Pod).
description: Big model weights on a Pod install download fast then drop to ~4 MB/s at 80-90% and crawl (old floor: seen at 63 KB/s on LTX's last 100MB). NOT verify/pip/decompress/custom-nodes (baked on Pod). Root cause: aria2c flags in wrapper.py = `-s 16 -k 16M` capped TOTAL splits at 16 → freed connections idle at the tail, final pieces dragged by 1-3 stragglers. FIX (two live passes): 0.2.28 `-s64 -k4M` lifted the BULK to ~533MB/s (proved the pipe is huge — NOT R2/NIC throttled) but the final ~270MB piece still ran on ONE connection (~6MB/s). 0.2.29 `-s128 -k1M` made -k (piece SIZE) the limiter so the final piece is ~1MB → tail floor raised to a STEADY ~11-14 MB/s (live-verified on LTX 57.8GB + Wan, fresh Pod, /health=0.2.29). Pod-only (local engine = single-stream node https, unaffected). Shipped via publish-runtime.sh (R2) — NO image rebuild. Original title 'move custom nodes to R2' was wrong premise (nodes baked on Pod); re-scoped. CEILING (accepted, do NOT re-open): aria2 STILL tapers at the very end — this is structural (last pieces on fewer connections), not a flag bug. User confirms 5-10 prior sessions could not flat-line it. `-s128 -k1M` raised the floor from a 63KB/s crawl to ~11-14 MB/s — the original complaint (crawl-to-halt) is materially fixed; chasing 14→bulk is diminishing returns. WRAPPER_VERSION 0.2.29. NOT committed (other agent commits shared wrapper.py + kanban).
